data.title: NOV Appoints Operating Chief Jose Bayardo to Be CEO
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

NOV named President and Chief Operating Officer Jose Bayardo as its next chief executive, effective Jan. 1.

Bayardo succeeds Clay Williams, who will retire on the same date, the energy solutions company said Wednesday.

Bayardo became president and operations chief earlier this year. He joined the company in 2015 as chief financial officer, and previously held roles at Continental Resources and Complete Production Services.

Williams has been CEO for more than a decade.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: XCel Brands, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
XCel Brands, Inc., a company specializing in the licensing and marketing of consumer brands, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting both challenges and strategic initiatives aimed at navigating the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenue: XCel Brands reported net revenue of $1.12 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, a decrease from $1.91 million in the same period in 2024. This decline was primarily attributed to a reduction in net licensing revenue.

Gross Profit: The company's gross profit for the quarter was $1.12 million, down from $1.51 million in the same period in 2024, indicating a decrease in profitability.

Operating Loss: XCel Brands recorded an operating loss of $(7.44) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(9.08) million in the same period in 2024, showing a reduction in operating losses.

Net Loss: The net loss for the quarter was $(7.99) million, compared to $(9.22) million in the same period in 2024, reflecting a slight improvement in net income.

Basic Earnings (Loss) Per Share: The basic loss per share was $(2.02) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(3.92) in the same period in 2024, indicating a reduction in loss per share.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: XCel Brands generates revenue primarily through licensing its brands. Significant contributions come from the Halston Master License, which accounted for 57% of total net revenue in the current quarter, and agreements with Qurate Retail Group, which contributed 21%.

New Product Launches: The company launched the TowerHill by Christie Brinkley brand in May 2024 and the LB70 by Lloyd Boston brand in August 2024. Additionally, the Trust, Respect, Love by Cesar Millan brand and GemmaMade, a collaboration with baking influencer Gemma Stafford, are planned for launch in the fourth quarter of 2025.

Geographical Performance: XCel Brands operates primarily in North America, managing its business activities on a consolidated basis. The company does not report intra-entity sales or transfers, focusing its operations within this region.

Future Outlook: Management has expressed concerns about the company's ability to meet financial obligations due to recurring losses and cash flow issues. Despite restructuring efforts and recent financing activities, there is substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern without additional funding.

Operational Strategy: XCel Brands continues to focus on a modern consumer products sales strategy, which includes interactive television, digital live-stream shopping, social commerce, and e-commerce channels, aiming to be present wherever its customers shop.

SEC Filing: XCel Brands, Inc. [ XELB ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: Wellgistics Health, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Wellgistics Health, Inc., a company specializing in distribution services, pharmacy retail sales, and third-party logistics services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report reveals significant financial and operational challenges faced by the company, including a notable decline in revenues and profitability, as well as concerns about its ability to continue as a going concern.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenues: The company reported net revenues of $3.01 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, a decrease from $5.67 million for the same period in 2024. This decline is attributed to reduced sales volume.

Gross Profit: Gross profit for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $0.23 million, down from $0.52 million in the same period in 2024, indicating a decline in profitability.

Loss from Operations: The company experienced a significant increase in loss from operations, reporting $(31.55) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(1.74) million for the same period in 2024. This increase is primarily due to higher operating expenses.

Net Loss: Net loss for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $(34.32) million, compared to $(1.87) million for the same period in 2024. The higher net loss is driven by increased operating expenses and interest costs.

Net Loss Per Common Share - Basic and Diluted: The net loss per common share was $(0.46) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.04) for the same period in 2024, reflecting the impact of the increased net loss on a per-share basis.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Wellgistics Health, Inc. reported revenue from three main segments: distribution services, pharmacy retail sales, and third-party logistics services. Distribution services generated $2,637,790 in revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025, while pharmacy retail sales and third-party logistics services contributed $172,100 and $203,014, respectively.

Geographical Performance: All revenues for the three and nine months ended September 30, 2025, were generated within the United States, indicating a domestic focus in the company's operations.

Future Outlook: Management expressed concerns about the company's ability to continue as a going concern due to significant net losses and cash used in operating activities. However, they highlighted potential capital from equity purchase agreements and public offerings as means to support ongoing operations and growth initiatives.

SEC Filing: Wellgistics Health, Inc. [ WGRX ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: NightFood Holdings, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
NightFood Holdings, Inc., a diversified company operating in the foodservice packaging, robotics, and hospitality sectors, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting its strategic shift towards technology-driven operations and new business segments.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenues - net: $0.782 million, reflecting the company's initial revenue-generating activities in its new business segments.
  • Loss from operations: $(2.166) million, indicating higher operating expenses compared to revenue.
  • Net loss: $(3.696) million, primarily due to high general and administrative expenses and other costs.
  • Net loss available to common stockholders: $(3.696) million, after accounting for deemed dividends on preferred stock.
  • Loss per share - basic and diluted: $(0.03), reflecting the impact of the net loss on shareholders.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: The company operates in three reportable segments: Foodservice Packaging Distribution, Robotics-as-a-Service (RaaS), and Hotel Operations. Each segment contributes to the company's revenue through distinct business activities.
  • Foodservice Packaging Distribution: Conducted through SWC Group, Inc., this segment focuses on the wholesale distribution of disposable foodservice packaging products, including printed paper cups, plastic cups, food containers, bags, and related consumables. Revenue is generated from the sale and shipment of products to customers.
  • Robotics-as-a-Service (RaaS): This segment, operated through Skytech Automated Solutions, Inc. and Future Hospitality Venture Holdings, Inc., provides automation solutions for foodservice and hospitality environments. Revenue is generated from fixed monthly service fees for the use of robotics equipment, remote monitoring, software services, and maintenance support.
  • Hotel Operations: This segment generates revenue from lodging and related guest services, including room rentals and ancillary offerings such as food, beverage, and other guest amenities. The hotels also serve as deployment and testing environments for the company’s automation technologies.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to expand into new and existing markets, with an emphasis on the RaaS model. It aims to obtain additional debt and/or equity financing to support working capital and growth, pursue strategic collaborations and partnerships, and selectively evaluate acquisitions that enhance or complement its business model.
  • Strategic Acquisitions: The company acquired two hotel properties on August 27, 2025, and September 30, 2025, as part of its strategy to establish a hospitality asset ownership and operations platform. These acquisitions are intended to support both revenue-generating hospitality operations and the deployment of the company’s automation technologies.
  • Hospitality Asset Ownership: The acquired hotels are intended to serve as revenue-generating hospitality operations and as dedicated deployment sites for the company’s automation technologies, enabling the company to test, validate, and scale operational efficiencies in live environments.
  • Business Model: The company offers its automation solutions under a Robotics-as-a-Service (RaaS) model, structured as multi-year lease and service agreements. Customers pay a recurring monthly fee for deployed equipment and related services, with revenue recognized on a monthly basis as services are rendered.
  • Integration and Operating Status: Following the acquisitions, the company initiated integration activities across both properties, including evaluations of site layouts, staffing structures, and operational workflows in preparation for automation deployments.
  • Discontinued Operations: The company has discontinued its legacy Snacks and Beverages business, which was not aligned with its current and expected future strategy. This decision reflects a strategic shift towards focusing on technology-driven operations.

SEC Filing: NightFood Holdings, Inc. [ NGTF ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: HOUSTON AMERICAN ENERGY CORP SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Houston American Energy Corp, a company engaged in legacy oil and gas operations and emerging renewable energy initiatives, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities, reflecting its strategic focus on maintaining traditional energy assets while expanding into the recycling and renewable energy sectors.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $0.2 million, reflecting the company's legacy oil and gas operations.
  • Net Loss: $(7.0) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.3) million for the same period in 2024, primarily due to increased general and administrative expenses related to the acquisition of AGIG.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share - Basic: $(0.21), compared to $(0.01) for the same period in 2024, reflecting the increased net loss.
  • Net Loss Per Common Share - Diluted: $(0.21), compared to $(0.01) for the same period in 2024, consistent with the basic loss per share due to anti-dilutive effects.

Business Highlights

  • Segment Performance: The company operates in two segments: legacy oil and gas (O&G) operations and emerging renewables initiatives. The O&G segment generates revenue from oil and gas operations, while the Renewables segment is in the pre-revenue stage, focusing on developing technologies for converting plastic and biomass waste into renewable fuels and chemicals.
  • Geographical Performance: Substantially all of the company's revenue and long-lived assets are located in Texas, indicating a concentrated geographical focus for its operations.
  • New Production Launches: The company completed the purchase of a 25-acre site at the Cedar Port Industrial Park in Baytown, Texas, for approximately $8.6 million. This site is intended for constructing the company's first plastics recycling plant, which will transform plastic waste into pyrolysis oil.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to maintain its legacy oil and gas assets while expanding its operations in the recycling and renewable energy sectors through its subsidiary, AGIG. The focus will be on using waste products to decarbonize the energy, fuels, and chemicals sector by providing renewable or recycled alternatives.

SEC Filing: HOUSTON AMERICAN ENERGY CORP [ HUSA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: Cactus Acquisition Corp. 1 Ltd SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Cactus Acquisition Corp. 1 Ltd, a company focused on merging with or acquiring businesses in the energy renewables sector, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2024. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and key business activities during the period.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Loss for the Period: $(359) thousand, reflecting a decrease from the previous period's net loss of $(306) thousand, primarily due to reduced interest income and increased operating expenses.
  • Basic and Diluted Earnings Per Class A Ordinary Share Subject to Possible Redemption: $0.58, an increase from $0.30 in the prior period, indicating improved earnings per share for redeemable shares.
  • Basic and Diluted Income (Loss) Per Non-Redeemable Class A and Class B Ordinary Share: $(0.26), a decrease from $1.23 in the prior period, reflecting a loss per share for non-redeemable shares.
  • Interest Earned on Marketable Securities Held in Trust Account: $283 thousand, a decrease from $840 thousand in the prior period, due to lower interest rates or reduced principal in the trust account.
  • Operating Expenses: $(542) thousand, a decrease from $(936) thousand in the prior period, indicating a reduction in operating costs.

Business Highlights

  • Business Combination Agreement: On August 29, 2024, Cactus Acquisition Corp. 1 Ltd signed a Business Combination Agreement with Tembo e-LV B.V., a private company in the Netherlands. This agreement is part of the company's strategy to merge with or acquire businesses in the energy renewables sector.
  • Geographical Focus Shift: Initially focused on technology-based healthcare businesses in Israel, the company shifted its focus to emerging technology companies globally, particularly in the energy renewables sector, following a change in sponsorship.
  • Extension Amendments: The company has extended its mandatory liquidation date multiple times, with the latest extension moving it to November 2, 2026. This extension allows more time to complete a business combination.
  • Delisting from Nasdaq: The company was delisted from Nasdaq due to not completing a business combination within the required timeframe. Trading of its securities commenced on the OTC market under the symbol CCTSFF.
  • Future Outlook: The company intends to complete an initial business combination before the mandatory liquidation date. However, there is substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern if it fails to secure additional financing or complete a business combination.
  • Promissory Notes: The company has issued several promissory notes to fund its operations, including a recent note to Hali International Limited for $150,000, indicating ongoing efforts to secure necessary funding for operational needs.
  • Operational Strategy: The company plans to use funds from its trust account, minus amounts paid to redeeming shareholders, to finance its initial business combination and subsequent operations of the target business.

SEC Filing: Cactus Acquisition Corp. 1 Ltd [ CCTSF ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: G20 countries' medium-term growth to be weakest since 2009 crisis, IMF says
data.description:
Key points:
  • G20 economies expected to grow 2.9% in 2030
  • Advanced G20 nations forecast to grow by 1.4% in 2030
  • G20 leaders to meet in South Africa this weekend
  • US, China, others skipping leaders' summit

By Andrea Shalal

The world's 20 largest economies will grow by just 2.9% in 2030 amid headwinds from protectionism and policy uncertainty, their weakest medium-term outlook since the global financial crisis of 2009, the International Monetary Fund said on Wednesday.

In a report to the Group of 20, the global lender mapped out a series of challenges facing the global economy, including widening excessive balances and stretched public coffers, as well as aging populations in advanced economies.

The G20's advanced economies - the United States, Britain, Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Italy, Japan and South Korea - were forecast to see economic growth of just 1.4% in 2030, it said. G20 emerging market economies - Argentina, Brazil, China, India, Indonesia, Mexico, Russia, Saudi Arabia, South Africa and Turkey - should see stronger growth of 3.9%, it said.

In 2025, the group's economic output was expected to expand by 3.2%, down from 3.3% last year, and moderating to 3.0% in 2026.

TRUMP, XI TO SKIP G20 MEETING

G20 leaders are due to meet in South Africa this weekend, minus U.S. President Donald Trump, Chinese President Xi Jinping, and the leaders of Argentina and Mexico.

The meeting comes after a year of trade tensions and hikes in effective tariff rates led by the U.S., where Trump is seeking to re-shore domestic manufacturing and end what he calls decades of unfair trading conditions for U.S. firms.

Disinflation was continuing, but headline inflation would still be around 3.5% in G20 countries in 2025, the IMF said.

It said global inflation was expected to continue to decline in the near term as demand waned and energy prices dropped, but rising tariffs posed a risk in economies that were implementing higher duties, like the U.S.

It said high-frequency indicators already pointed to rising producer prices in the U.S. and rising core inflation. U.S. core inflation was not expected to return to the Federal Reserve's 2% target until 2027, two years later than predicted in last year's IMF report to the G20, it said.

The IMF urged countries to cooperate to lower trade barriers and reduce uncertainty that was weighing on growth prospects, encouraging G20 members to adopt "clear and transparent trade policy road maps."

Trade deals should avoid purchase commitments and quantitative restrictions, the IMF said, without referencing any specific deals signed by the U.S. and a host of its trading partners in recent months, many of which included purchase commitments.

data.title: China International Capital Plans to Acquire Brokerage Firms Cinda Securities, Dongxing Securities
Wall Street indexes end rocky session higher; Nvidia gains after hours
data.description:
Key points:
  • Target drops after missing quarterly comparable sales estimates
  • Indexes briefly pare gains after Fed minutes
  • September US jobs report due on Thursday
  • Indexes: Dow up 0.1%, S&P 500 up 0.4%, Nasdaq up 0.6%

By Caroline Valetkevitch

U.S. stocks ended a choppy session higher on Wednesday, bouncing off recent losses as technology stocks rose ahead of Nvidia's quarterly results.

Nvidia shares gained more than 5% after ending the regular session up 2.8% as the artificial intelligence leader forecast fourth-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates. Shares of Advanced Micro Devices rose 2.8% after the bell, while Alphabet was up 1.6% and Palantir Technologies was up 4%.

"The market is breathing a big, collective sigh of relief because Nvidia is confirming that AI demand is strong," said Adam Sarhan, chief executive of 50 Park Investments in New York.

"If it sticks and stays by tomorrow's close ... this little pullback in the market could end." The S&P 500 is still down more than 3% from its October highs.

Indexes briefly pared gains during the regular session after minutes from the last Federal Reserve meeting showed policymakers cautioned that lower borrowing costs could undermine the fight against inflation. The Fed cut rates by a quarter of a percentage point at each of its meetings in September and October.

Worries about a cooling labor market persisted ahead of Thursday's release of the September U.S. jobs report following the record-long U.S. government shutdown. The U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics said it will not publish the October employment report, but will instead combine nonfarm payrolls for that month with November's report.

Nvidia's results have been seen as a test for the AI-driven rally that has powered the market to record highs this year.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 47.03 points, or 0.10%, to 46,138.77, the S&P 500 gained 24.84 points, or 0.38%, to 6,642.16 and the Nasdaq Composite advanced 131.38 points, or 0.59%, to 22,564.23.

Investors have worried about how companies will be able to make money from their huge investments in AI.

Among decliners, Target shares fell 2.8% after the retailer reported a bigger-than-expected drop in quarterly sales as cash-strapped U.S. consumers cut back on discretionary spending.

Shares of Walmart , which is due to report earnings on Thursday before the bell, ended 0.8% lower.

Declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.59-to-1 ratio on the NYSE, where there were 55 new highs and 211 new lows.

On the Nasdaq, 1,846 stocks rose and 2,851 fell, as declining issues outnumbered advancers by a 1.54-to-1 ratio.

The S&P 500 posted 12 new 52-week highs and 22 new lows while the Nasdaq Composite recorded 48 new highs and 305 new lows.

Volume on U.S. exchanges was 17.46 billion shares, compared with the roughly 20.2 billion average for the full session over the last 20 trading days.

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Mich. coal plant to continue operations through winter after latest DOE order
data.description: (Platts)--19Nov2025/529 pm EST/2229 GMT **DOE cites low winter energy reserves for extension **Extends previously existing emergency order A retirement-ready coal plant in Michigan will continue to operate after the Trump administration issued another three-month emergency order to keep the facility open. The extension was expected. In a third US Department of Energy emergency order, which was issued after the close of business on Nov. 18, US Energy Secretary Chris Wright cited North American Electric Reliability Corp. assessments indicating winter energy reserves may be low during extreme conditions. NERC issued its 2025-2026 report on Nov. 18, projecting a sharp increase of 2.5% in winter peak demand over the next several months — but it said reliability risks were limited in the MISO region. "MISO was able to procure 6.1% more resources through the annual planning reserve auction than required by its minimum resource adequacy target," NERC said. "A further 3.3 GW of resources were available but not chosen to be committed for the winter season." CMS Energy's 1,410 MW J.H. Campbell plant on the Lake Michigan shoreline had been scheduled to shut down May 31, but Wright on May 23 told the utility to call off the closure. Wright said at that time that the plant was needed to keep the Midcontinent ISO stable during the hot summer months and prevent potentially dangerous outages. In a lawsuit pending in the US Court of Appeals for the District of Columbia, Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel and clean energy groups alleged the emergency assessment was "fabricated." Several environmental groups made similar claims in a second Campbell plant lawsuit that is moving through the same court. At the time the Michigan lawsuit was filed, a DOE spokesperson cited President Donald Trump's Jan. 20 executive order declaring a national energy emergency as a justification for the retirement delay. CMS Energy said in October that keeping the Campbell plant running cost the company $80 million during the first two emergency periods. The utility expects to file for a cost recovery with the US Federal Energy Regulatory Commission by the end of 2025, with MISO ratepayers picking up the tab. — Karin Rives, karin.rives@spglobal.com-- Edited by Richard Rubin, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Awaysis Capital, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Awaysis Capital, Inc., a company specializing in the management and development of resort properties, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights the company's financial performance and operational activities, reflecting its ongoing transition from a shell company to an active player in the hospitality and real estate sectors.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: $95,198

The increase in revenue from the three months ended September 30, 2024, to 2025 was a result of increased booking and rental revenues, management fee income related to the completion of two buildings at Casamora, and the acquisition of Chial Mountain Limited.

Operating Income: $(639,468)

Operating losses were primarily driven by operating expenses incurred as the company continued to scale its hospitality operations under the Awaysis brand and advanced preparations for a registered offering of its securities.

Net Loss: $(651,548)

The decrease in net loss from the three months ended September 30, 2024, to 2025 was principally driven by increased revenue generated from the addition of Chial Mountain Limited and related rental and management income.

Basic and diluted net loss per common share: $(0.00)

This reflects the company's ongoing transition from being a shell company into an operating business.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments:

The company generates revenue primarily from short-term unit rentals, management services, and real estate sales. These activities are focused on residential vacation home communities in desirable travel destinations.

Geographical Performance:

The company's operations are primarily based in Belize, where it manages and develops resort properties. The focus is on transforming resort properties into residential-resort enclaves, capitalizing on global trends towards remote work and extended stays.

Sales Units:

The company is involved in the sale of developed resort inventory, including condominiums and villas, as part of its broader development and growth strategy.

Future Outlook:

The company plans to expand its inventory of units and marketing footprint over time, with a focus on compliance with legal and regulatory requirements in all jurisdictions of operation. It aims to enhance its operational infrastructure and invest in branding and marketing initiatives to support growth.

SEC Filing: Awaysis Capital, Inc. [ AWCA ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: American Strategic Investment Co. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
American Strategic Investment Co., a company primarily focused on managing office properties in Manhattan, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and operational challenges amid a difficult market environment influenced by the COVID-19 pandemic.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue from tenants: $36.8 million, reflecting a decrease compared to the previous year due to lower rental rates and occupancy challenges.
  • Operating loss before gain on disposition of real estate investments: $(39.6) million, indicating continued operational challenges prior to asset sales.
  • Net income (loss) and Net income (loss) attributable to common stockholders: $(14.5) million, showing improvement from the previous year's loss due to gains on asset sales.
  • Net income (loss) per share attributable to common stockholders — Basic: $(5.70), reflecting the overall net loss for the period.
  • Net income (loss) per share attributable to common stockholders — Diluted: $(5.70), consistent with the basic earnings per share.

Business Highlights

  • Geographical Performance: The company's portfolio consists primarily of office properties located in Manhattan, which have been adversely impacted by shifts in market demand following the COVID-19 pandemic. The New York City office market continues to face challenges as leasing and occupancy trends have slowed, affecting the company's revenue streams.
  • Operational Challenges: The company has faced significant liquidity constraints due to sustained declines in rental income, constrained cash flow from operations, and ongoing debt service obligations. These challenges are compounded by the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the commercial real estate market.
  • Property Management: The company owns six properties consisting of 0.7 million rentable square feet, excluding one property in a consensual foreclosure process. The management of these properties is conducted through the company's advisor and property manager, both under common control with AR Global Investments, LLC.
  • Future Outlook: Management has developed a plan to alleviate substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern. This includes paying related party fees in shares, selling one of the company's performing assets within the next twelve months, and the advisor's willingness to lend funds if necessary.
  • Tenant and Lease Management: The company has successfully executed lease transactions to mitigate vacancy risk, although new leases have been executed at market rental rates below prior contractual rates, resulting in decreased revenue compared to historical levels.
  • Impairment Charges: The company recorded significant impairment charges on several properties, including 1140 Avenue of the Americas, 400 E. 67th Street/200 Riverside, and 196 Orchard Street, to reduce their carrying values to estimated fair values.
  • Property Disposition: During the reporting period, the company lost its economic interest and control over the 1140 Avenue of the Americas property due to a consensual foreclosure process, resulting in the removal of related assets and liabilities from the balance sheet.
  • Lease and Revenue Recognition: The company continues to recognize revenue from lease contracts on a straight-line basis over the initial term of the lease, with a weighted-average remaining lease term of 6.2 years as of September 30, 2025.

SEC Filing: American Strategic Investment Co. [ NYC ] - 10-Q - Nov. 19, 2025

data.title: EARN: Net investment income increased to $8.5M as the CLO portfolio expanded and NAV reached $5.99
data.description:
NAV per share was $5.99 with $0.24 in distributions; net investment income rose to $8.5M ($0.23/share) as the CLO portfolio grew to $379.6M. Management expects continued opportunities from loan repricing and credit dispersion.

Original document: Ellington Credit Company [EARN] SEC 8-K Current Report — Nov. 20 2025
